turns.content: I just finished Gone Girl. Crime fiction is my absolute favorite genre.
Gone Girl is a great read! The twists are relentless.
I am already looking for my next crime fiction book. Any suggestions?
Try The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo if you have not read it yet.
I picked up a mystery novel called The Thursday Murder Club. Loving it so far.
That one is charming and funny! Richard Osman is a great writer.
I had not read much mystery before but now I am hooked. Very different from crime fiction.
Mystery and crime fiction do overlap a lot but mystery tends to be cozier.
My book club assigned Dune this month. I had never read science fiction before.
Dune is a classic! It is quite dense but deeply rewarding.
I am really enjoying it. Might read more science fiction after this.
The Foundation series by Asimov is a great follow-up if you enjoy Dune.
I just started Wolf Hall. Historical fiction is surprisingly gripping.
Hilary Mantel is extraordinary. Wolf Hall won the Booker Prize for good reason.
I love how it brings Tudor England to life. So immersive.
It is a masterpiece. You have now read across four very different fiction genres!
